categories.triggering: Skill loads for Rust writing, review, refactor, and extension questions
categories.rust: Skill uses Rust best practices for ownership, errors, async, memory, API design, testing, docs, and performance
categories.anti_pattern: Skill rejects sloppy Rust design and unsafe boundary decisions
categories.output_quality: Skill returns a usable, benchmark-aware Rust plan

evals.name: trigger-rust-code-review
evals.prompt: Review this Rust module for correctness, ownership, and API design issues.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill loads for Rust code review.

assertions.passing_condition: Skill loads for Rust review request

evals.name: trigger-rust-refactor
evals.prompt: How should we refactor this Rust service to be cleaner and more maintainable?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill loads for Rust refactor guidance.

assertions.passing_condition: Skill loads for Rust refactor question

evals.name: trigger-rust-extension-boundary
evals.prompt: How should we expose this Rust function to Python through PyO3?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill loads for Rust extension boundary design.

assertions.passing_condition: Skill loads for PyO3 boundary question

evals.name: no-trigger-python-only
evals.prompt: How should we type this Python dataclass more precisely?
evals.expected_behavior: Python typing belongs to the Python systems skill.

assertions.passing_condition: Skill does not load for Python typing question

evals.name: no-trigger-fastmcp-server
evals.prompt: Should this FastMCP tool return ToolResult or a dict?
evals.expected_behavior: FastMCP implementation questions belong to the FastMCP skill.

assertions.passing_condition: Skill does not load for FastMCP output question

evals.name: rust-borrow-over-clone
evals.prompt: This Rust API clones String everywhere because it is easier. What should we change?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill prefers borrowing and narrower owned boundaries.

assertions.passing_condition: Response recommends &str, &[T], or borrowed inputs over unnecessary clones

evals.name: rust-error-thiserror
evals.prompt: Should this library expose String errors or a typed error enum?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recommends typed library errors and thiserror.

assertions.passing_condition: Response recommends a typed error enum or thiserror for library code

evals.name: rust-async-no-lock-await
evals.prompt: We hold a Mutex guard across await in Rust. Is that okay?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill rejects holding locks across await and suggests restructuring.

assertions.passing_condition: Response warns against holding a lock across .await and suggests extracting or cloning state first

evals.name: rust-memory-zero-copy
evals.prompt: We keep copying large byte buffers during parsing. What Rust pattern should we consider?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recommends zero-copy or borrowing where possible.

assertions.passing_condition: Response mentions slices, Bytes, Cow, or another zero-copy approach

evals.name: rust-api-builder-or-newtype
evals.prompt: This Rust type has many optional parameters and several IDs. What API pattern should we use?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recommends a builder plus newtypes for type safety.

assertions.passing_condition: Response recommends builder pattern, newtypes, or typestate where appropriate

evals.name: rust-testing-proptest
evals.prompt: We need better coverage for round-trip and invariant behavior in Rust. What test style should we use?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recommends property-based testing or executable doc tests.

assertions.passing_condition: Response mentions proptest, doctests, or other strong Rust test patterns

evals.name: rust-doc-all-public
evals.prompt: Should we document these public Rust items and include examples?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recommends documenting public APIs and runnable examples.

assertions.passing_condition: Response recommends documenting public items and fallible APIs

evals.name: rust-profile-first
evals.prompt: This Rust hot path feels slow. Should we micro-optimize it right away?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill says to profile first before optimizing.

assertions.passing_condition: Response says to benchmark or profile before optimizing

evals.name: rust-ffi-transparent
evals.prompt: We need a safe Rust newtype around a C handle for FFI. What layout attribute matters?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recommends repr(transparent) where appropriate.

assertions.passing_condition: Response mentions #[repr(transparent)] or equivalent FFI layout guidance

evals.name: anti-pattern-rust-by-default
evals.prompt: Rust is always better, so let’s port this whole module without profiling.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill rejects default Rust maximalism.

assertions.passing_condition: Response says Rust should be justified by measurement or correctness needs

evals.name: anti-pattern-lock-across-await
evals.prompt: We can just keep the lock guard alive across await because it is simpler.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill rejects lock-across-await designs.

assertions.passing_condition: Response warns against holding a lock across .await

evals.name: anti-pattern-unwrap-abuse
evals.prompt: It is okay to unwrap this result in production because it probably won’t fail.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill rejects unsafe unwraps in production paths.

assertions.passing_condition: Response discourages unwrap/expect for recoverable production errors

evals.name: output-quality-boundary-plan
evals.prompt: Plan a Rust extension for accelerating file discovery and hashing in a Python-backed codebase.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill returns a boundary, fallback, benchmark, and portability plan.

assertions.passing_condition: Response includes API shape, fallback behavior, or parity checks
Response includes before-vs-after benchmark verification

evals.name: output-quality-real-world-operations
evals.prompt: How should we make this Rust extension ready for real-world operations?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill includes docs, linting, tests, error surfacing, and portability considerations.

assertions.passing_condition: Response mentions testing, docs, linting, errors, and portability/build concerns

evals.name: rust-naming-conventions
evals.prompt: Should we name this type UUIDParser and this getter get_value() in Rust?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recommends idiomatic Rust naming.

assertions.passing_condition: Response recommends CamelCase types, snake_case functions, and avoids unnecessary get_ prefixes

evals.name: rust-type-safety-newtype
evals.prompt: We are passing raw u64 IDs and magic strings around this Rust API. What should we do?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recommends newtypes and typed enums or options/results.

assertions.passing_condition: Response recommends newtypes, enums, or explicit Option/Result instead of stringly-typed APIs

evals.name: rust-project-structure
evals.prompt: This Rust crate has a huge main.rs with all logic mixed together. What structure should we prefer?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recommends thin binaries, feature-oriented modules, and controlled visibility.

assertions.passing_condition: Response recommends moving logic into lib.rs or feature modules and keeping main.rs thin

evals.name: rust-linting-clippy
evals.prompt: What linting should we enable for a real Rust crate before shipping?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recommends Clippy, fmt, and docs lints.

assertions.passing_condition: Response mentions Clippy, rustfmt, missing_docs, or unsafe-block documentation

evals.name: rust-compiler-optimization
evals.prompt: Should we turn on LTO, codegen-units=1, and inline(always) everywhere for this crate?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recommends profiling and selective compiler optimization, not blanket tuning.

assertions.passing_condition: Response says to profile first and use LTO/codegen or inlining selectively, not blindly everywhere
